question_text: Some large-scale manufacturers have retail shops of their own ____.

options.C: before a new product is sold all over the country
options.D: unless some firms carry out regular surveys throughout the region
options.E: though sometimes the advice of an agency was sought on the packaging of the article

question_text: Kerrich carried out exhaustive experiments in probability ____.

options.C: whether the coin toss is truly random
options.D: while he was interned in a camp in Jutland for the duration of the war
options.E: that he has been acclaimed as one of South Africa’s leading mathematicians

question_text: The manual workers in the automobile factory are threatening to go on strike ____.

options.A: if they do not receive an adequate pay rise
options.B: after their pay claim is granted
options.C: that working conditions are not improving fast enough
options.D: even if the union hadn’t offered any support
options.E: which is due to start next week

question_text: The evacuation of the World Trade Center towers might have been easier ____.

options.D: so long as effective fire-proofing had been installed
options.E: after so many of the offices had already been vacated

question_text: After he had learned my side of the story from Molly, ____.

options.C: I don’t even want to listen to his apologies
options.D: he’s still too proud to admit he behaved badly
options.E: it will obviously be better to forget all about it

question_text: Despite the great concern for leadership and the large volume of writings about it, ____.

options.D: older children would actually follow his lead

question_text: Though every industrial development project is a potential source of pollution ____.

options.A: air pollution would clearly have been the most dangerous of all

options.D: such benefits as reductions in crop losses would help to offset the costs of pollution control

question_text: ____ that basic evolutionary processes in the future will differ substantially from those in the past.

options.B: Nobody had thought to ask themselves
options.C: They are carrying out a study of island biogeography
options.D: There is no reason whatsoever to assume

question_text: ____ despite the claim of his lawyers that his statements had been made under “highly coercive conditions”.

options.C: John Walker, the American Taliban, came from a humble background
options.D: John Walker was indicted by a federal grand jury

question_text: Though some scholars argue that ____, others say that the beginning of the 15th century was its real rise.

options.A: by the late part of the 12th century most of the literary conventions of Urdu literature have already been well established
options.B: the 12th century was the time of the great religious movements in India
options.C: Urdu literature dates back to the 12th century
options.D: most of the literary writings in India in the 12th century consisted of religious tracts and allegories
options.E: most Urdu poets in the 12th century turned to romances and narratives of heroic deeds

question_text: ____, to whom they report.

options.A: A growing body of evidence does indeed suggest that Enron was a case of bad management
options.B: In theory, a company’s auditors are appointed independently by its shareholders
options.C: The Enron fiasco has shown that all is not well with the governance of many big American companies

question_text: ____ where they ate mosquito larvae and so helped prevent malaria.

options.A: British engineers regarded these canals as “dond rivers”

options.C: Indeed irrigation gave a much-needed boost to declining agricultural production
options.D: The dam prevents all silt from flowing downstream

question_text: Though coral reefs are among the richest ecosystems on the planet, ____.

options.A: they grow in crystal-clear waters that contain hardly any nutrients
options.B: nutrient uptake is closely related to the degree of movement in the sea
options.C: the rough surfaces are what make this possible
options.D: they are also skilled at recycling
options.E: changes in turbulence accurately predicted the rate of nutrient uptake

question_text: ____, there just wasn’t enough evidence to convict him.

options.A: If the trial had been postponed to a later date
options.B: Since almost nothing the accused himself said could be substantiate
options.C: Though he seemed extremely reluctant to sign the statement
options.D: Even before the judge has finished summing up the case
options.E: Although everyone was convinced that Smith had committed the murder

question_text: In his annual report, the accountant failed to clarify the position and explain ____.

options.A: why expenditure had been in excess of the budget
options.B: whether the budget really is unrealistic
options.C: what procedures will have been used in banking
options.D: that more serious aspects were being overlooked
options.E: which items in particular are being reconsidered

question_text: Environmentalists are not often fond of dams ____.

options.C: unless the dams actually held back a vast sedimentation load
options.D: since they frequently trap migratory fish

question_text: Today more than 100 million people have a breathing problem ____.

options.B: that asthma levels are dropping in those born after 1985
options.C: which costs $6 billion a year in the US alone
options.D: unless they prefer not to admit it
options.E: since exposure to antibiotics while in the womb is generally regarded as a further irritant

question_text: The clarinet solo at the start of the movement provides the theme ____.

options.D: until yet another ecstatic climax was reached
options.E: that the work begins quickly and harmoniously
options.C: once the slow tempo had been re-established
options.A: if the sense of longing were once more brought to the force

question_text: Unlike most animals, the baby elephant very often has to be up on its feet and moving with the herd____.

options.A: whether it is fully functional straightaway
options.B: so it has an amazing ability to learn and remember things.
options.C: as if there were a long period of juvenile dependency

question_text: ____, urban farms are usually far more productive than their rural counterparts.

question_text: It has only recently been accepted ____.

question_text: ____ as one does not know the contents of the next chapter!

options.D: The speech aroused no response whatsoever in the audience

question_text: If only the tax return over the year had been as high as had been predicted, ______.
